text: Maps are quiet acts of translation, turning the vastness of the world into something a hand can hold and an eye can follow. Every map is also a confession of what its maker chose to keep and what to leave out, for no map can hold everything and remain a map. We trust them anyway, tracing their thin blue rivers and patient borders. Perhaps that is their deepest charm: they promise that even the unknown can be drawn, named, and, in time, gently understood.
